findings.description: u_0002 describes a distinct, mandatory image-treatment rule (images must be housed in the Accent shape — asymmetric rounded-corner crops, not plain rectangles/circles) but no candidate rule captures it. This unit is entirely missing from the cluster, leaving a governance gap for a clearly stated constraint.

payload.statement: Images must be housed in the Accent shape (§2.5) — asymmetric rounded-corner crops; plain rectangles or circles are prohibited.

findings.description: u_0002 explicitly cross-references '§2.5' (the Accent shape spec) — a relation to another rule/section that is not represented anywhere in the candidate rule set. Even if the missing rule (f_lisraya_01) is added, its relation to the §2.5 shape definition should be modeled explicitly.

The alt text rule is flagged in-source as a 'brand accessibility rule' — a compliance-oriented statement — yet the extracted rule carries no governance facets (e.g., accessibility standard reference such as WCAG, owning team/policy, enforcement mechanism, or escalation for non-compliance). It is treated as a plain imagery style rule instead of a compliance obligation.

Hardness 'strong_default' paired with polarity 'must' is internally inconsistent — 'strong_default' typically implies a preferred/default choice with allowed exceptions (should), whereas 'must' implies an absolute requirement (hard_constraint). Source text ('Photography: warm, realistic patient lifestyle imagery — real moments...') reads as a definitional/default guideline rather than an absolute mandate, suggesting polarity should be 'should' if hardness stays strong_default, or hardness should become hard_constraint if 'must' is retained.
